title: CS50's Introduction to Programming with Python
description: Harvard's intro to programming using Python — good first step if you are new to code or want structured fundamentals.

title: FastAPI documentation & tutorial
description: Official guide to building APIs with Python and FastAPI, including async patterns and OpenAPI.

title: MDN JavaScript guide
description: Deep, vendor-neutral JavaScript fundamentals from Mozilla — ideal for solidifying web skills.

title: React — Learn
description: Interactive official tutorial for building UIs with React and modern JavaScript.

title: SQLBolt
description: Short, interactive lessons on SQL queries — tables, joins, and aggregations.

title: AWS Skill Builder — Cloud essentials
description: Free digital training for core AWS services and cloud concepts (filter for free content).

title: Machine Learning (Andrew Ng)
description: Classic introduction to ML concepts, linear models, and neural networks.

title: System Design Primer
description: Large open-source repo covering scalability, trade-offs, and interview-style system design.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications (book)
description: In-depth look at databases, replication, and distributed systems — strong follow-on after basics.

title: Kubernetes Basics (official)
description: Official interactive tutorial for pods, deployments, and services on Kubernetes.

title: LeetCode (interview prep)
description: Practice coding problems by topic — pair with a curated pattern list for interviews.

title: Deep Learning Specialization
description: Neural networks, CNNs, sequence models — for after introductory ML.

title: PostgreSQL documentation
description: Official docs for SQL features, indexing, and performance — complements hands-on SQL practice.

title: A Tour of Go
description: Interactive introduction to Go syntax, concurrency, and standard patterns.

title: The Rust Programming Language
description: Official book for ownership, borrowing, and systems programming in Rust.

title: freeCodeCamp — full curriculum
description: Free paths in web, data, and tools that align with common technology-industry roles.

title: OpenWHO — health learning
description: WHO open courses on public health, outbreak readiness, and health systems — useful context for healthcare-focused tech roles.

title: Khan Academy — finance & capital markets
description: Core ideas in markets, interest, and financial instruments — helpful grounding for finance and fintech adjacent engineering.

title: Learning How to Learn (Coursera)
description: Evidence-based study techniques — strong fit for education-sector roles and lifelong learning habits.

title: Digital Manufacturing & Design (Coursera)
description: Industry 4.0, digital threads, and smart manufacturing concepts common in manufacturing organizations.

title: Shopify Learn — retail & commerce
description: Retail operations, ecommerce, and customer experience fundamentals relevant to retail tech.

title: eCornell — hospitality courses
description: Service operations, revenue, and guest experience from a leading hospitality program (many courses are paid; browse for fit).

title: Supply Chain Management (Coursera)
description: Planning, sourcing, logistics, and delivery — core to transportation and supply-chain heavy industries.

title: Google Technical Writing courses
description: Two short courses on clear structure, style, and documentation aimed at engineers — also ideal for anyone who writes specs, wikis, or user-facing copy.

title: GOV.UK — Content design guidance
description: Public-sector gold standard for planning, structuring, and testing written content so users can complete tasks.

title: PlainLanguage.gov — Federal plain language guidelines
description: Practical guidance for clear, concise writing for broad audiences — useful for policy, comms, and internal docs.

title: Good with Words: Writing and Editing (Michigan)
description: Foundations in word choice, sentence flow, and drafting — strong for reports, marketing, and professional writing.

title: The Scrum Guide
description: Official definition of Scrum — roles, events, and artifacts. Lightweight entry to agile delivery for PMs and cross-functional teams.

title: Atlassian — Agile project management
description: Overviews of Scrum, Kanban, roadmaps, and rituals — approachable for PMs and leads without a programming focus.

title: Digital Product Management (Darden / Coursera)
description: Ideation, strategy, and roadmapping for digital products — fits product, program, and business-focused roles.

title: Google Project Management Professional Certificate
description: Foundations in project lifecycles, stakeholder comms, and Agile — widely used for non-engineering PM career paths (Coursera; audit for free).

title: Nielsen Norman Group — UX research & writing articles
description: Evidence-based articles on usability testing, UX writing, and design thinking — valuable for PM, content, and research-adjacent roles.

title: IDEO U — Design thinking introduction
description: Human-centered problem framing and prototyping — complements PM and strategy work without requiring code.

title: Inspired (product leadership) — SVPG resources
description: Essays and book pointers on empowered product teams, discovery, and outcomes — advanced lens after basic PM coursework.

title: Agile Practice Guide (PMI overview)
description: How predictive and agile approaches blend in larger programs — useful for PMs moving into scaled delivery (summary resources; full guide often via PMI).
